title: Nvidia and Sega renew their alliance: Sega games will arrive on RTX Spark PCs
summary: The historic partnership between Nvidia and Sega is reborn with compatibility of games like Virtua Fighter Crossroads on the new RTX Spark PCs.

title: Thinking Machines Lab launches its first open AI model, Inkling
summary: The startup Thinking Machines presents Inkling, an open language model that competes with other generalist AI models, available for public download.

title: Intel is the first to manufacture high-volume chips with ASML's High NA EUV lithography for its Panther Lake processors
summary: Intel uses ASML's high numerical aperture extreme ultraviolet lithography machine to produce select layers of its Panther Lake chips, marking a milestone in the industry.

title: Meta employees sue over AI-assisted layoffs they consider discriminatory
summary: Twenty-six Meta workers file a class action lawsuit alleging that the artificial intelligence system used to select layoffs was discriminatory.

title: Palit relaunches the GeForce RTX 3060 graphics card with 12 GB of VRAM as a solution to the AI shortage
summary: Palit is reselling the RTX 3060, a 2021 GPU with 12 GB of memory, to alleviate the shortage of hardware for artificial intelligence.

title: Starlink launches its smaller and lighter V5 residential kit with speeds up to 375 Mbps
summary: The new Starlink V5 antenna halves the size and energy consumption compared to the V4, offering greater speed and efficiency for the home.

title: A user creates physical cartridges for Steam games using 2.5-inch SSDs
summary: An enthusiast designs a cartridge system for Steam games stored on 128 GB SSDs that auto-boot when connected.

title: Stripe and Advent have reportedly offered to acquire PayPal for around $53.4 billion
summary: The companies Stripe and Advent have made a joint offer to buy PayPal, valuing the company at approximately $53.4 billion.

title: Valve stops manufacturing spare parts for the Steam Deck LCD, complicating repairs
summary: Valve removes from the market replacement components for the Steam Deck LCD, making it more complicated and expensive to repair the device.

title: Apple Intelligence gets approval to launch in China with Alibaba's Qwen AI
summary: Apple receives the green light to implement its artificial intelligence system in China, using Alibaba's technology to comply with local regulations.

title: Bonsai 27B: the complete open reasoning model that fits on an iPhone
summary: The Bonsai 27B artificial intelligence model, with 27 billion parameters, can run entirely on an iPhone, democratizing access to advanced AI.

title: Google's largest clean energy project is located 40 miles north of xAI's unpermitted gas plant
summary: Google pushes its largest renewable energy initiative while xAI faces criticism for operating a gas plant without the necessary permits.

title: GPT-5.6 Sol solves a 30-year statistical conjecture in 90 minutes, something humans had not achieved
summary: OpenAI's new AI model demonstrated the falsehood of a statistical conjecture that had challenged mathematicians for three decades.

title: Microsoft fixes a record number of security vulnerabilities and attributes the achievement to the use of artificial intelligence
summary: The tech company claims that AI has been key to identifying and patching more security flaws than ever in a single update cycle.

title: OpenAI unveils its first hardware product: a screenless AI speaker that aims to feel alive
summary: The company launches a smart speaker without a screen that uses AI to interact naturally and expressively with users.

title: OpenAI employees fund a rival super PAC to oppose their CEO
summary: A group of OpenAI workers creates an independent political action committee to oppose the company's management decisions.

title: SpaceX lowers its IPO price to $135 before Starship launch
summary: Elon Musk's aerospace company adjusts its share value for the initial public offering as it prepares for the next flight of its Starship rocket.

title: A cyberattack suggests that AI music generator Suno used YouTube to obtain training data
summary: Analysis of a hack reveals that Suno may have extracted content from YouTube without permission to train its music generation models.

title: NTSB confirms that the Tesla driver in the fatal Texas accident pressed the accelerator 100%
summary: The National Transportation Safety Board report reveals that the accelerator was fully pressed in the fatal Tesla accident in Texas.

title: US accuses Russian hosting providers of cyberattacks that generated $62 million in illicit profits
summary: US authorities file charges against Russian web hosting services known for harboring cybercriminal activities without intervention.
